effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the six-file change is bounded, but lifecycle-first status election across committed and uncommitted candidates is a subtle state-authority boundary.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator or automation caller can discover and open every locally available release board without checking out each ref or knowing its release name in advance.

positive_scenarios: The releases pane lists and opens a release whose plan is absent from HEAD but present on its selected release-worktree ref.
The TUI displays a higher lifecycle state from a valid matching uncommitted checkout file even when sourceRef names another branch.
negative_scenarios: A catalog error cannot silently show a partial list or an unrelated filesystem board.
A lower, malformed, or identity-mismatched live status cannot regress the Oracle baseline, and a later attention state cannot be hidden by an older success.
An asynchronous board result from a different source ref cannot replace the user's current selection.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: The TUI becomes an accurate operational front end for release plans that Baton has materialised in sibling worktrees or remote-tracking refs, including progress not yet committed by the current operator.

design_decisions.choice: Elect the farthest advanced valid current-checkout status over the committed Oracle baseline rather than treating selected source-ref identity as state authority.
options: lifecycle-first comparison of committed Oracle baseline and matching current checkout
unbounded timestamp-first scan of all refs and worktrees
design_decisions.human_decision: Repository owner chose lifecycle-first current-checkout arbitration on 2026-07-17.
design_decisions.rationale: Uncommitted real progress must be visible, but the candidate set stays bounded and explicit newer attention states remain fail-closed visible.
